Investor AnatomyHome

We map the Federal Reserve Watcher as an explicit analyst pipeline

The Federal Reserve Watcher is not a single report page. FRED, COT, and BLS modules feed their actual signals directly into the IA macro themes, then the macro, tone, Beige Book, projections, and validation modules converge in the final decision engine.

This Fed Watcher Analyst now mirrors that direct source-to-signal-to-theme wiring. Red nodes highlight the confirmed implementation risks: the macro stage still reads April 1, 2026 legacy macro-theme HTML instead of the April 4, 2026 IA pages, Global Trade & Demand still is not consumed by the macro watcher, and document caches are effectively write-once.

Model Architecture

How FRED, COT, and BLS sources feed their actual signals into each IA macro theme, and how those themes flow into the live Federal Reserve Watcher. This view keeps the macro wiring explicit while still flagging the stale or fragile parts.

1. Build macro context

FRED, COT, and BLS feed the named macro signals directly into the IA theme pages.

2. Synthesize policy intent

Macro, tone, Beige Book, projections, and document validation flow into the Federal Reserve Watcher decision engine.

3. Publish outputs, expose fragility

The Federal Reserve Watcher and stored podcast payload remain visible alongside the stale macro wiring and cache limits.
